section.title: 7.1 Description
section.p: The retrieval engine uses multiple signals when ranking candidate chunks.
These signals allow the system to improve relevance beyond simple vector similarity.

section.title: 7.2 Vector Similarity
section.p: Semantic similarity between the user query embedding and the chunk embedding.

section.title: 7.3 Keyword Matching
section.p: Semantic similarity between the user query embedding and the chunk embedding.

section.title: 7.4 Hierarchy Signals
section.p: Structural signals derived from document titles and headings.
These signals help prioritize content that appears in higher-level sections of a document.

section.title: 7.5 Metadata Signals
section.p: Metadata signals are actively used during retrieval scoring.
Metadata contributes to ranking through weighted scoring bonuses applied during hybrid scoring.

li.p: enhance retrieval precision beyond semantic similarity

section.title: 7.6 Intent Signals
section.p: The system incorporates query intent as a dynamic retrieval signal.
Intent influences ranking by adjusting metadata-based scoring weights.

li.p: experience queries prioritize resume and project history
technical queries prioritize architecture and pipeline documentation

section.p: This enables context-aware retrieval behavior without requiring additional model inference.
